mnemonics: FACTS DIMensions: Facts are numbers, DIMs describe them.
Star Schema: Facts are the center, Dimensions are the points.
keyPoints: Separate quantitative data (facts) from descriptive data (dimensions).
The Star Schema denormalizes dimensions for faster query performance.
Grain defines the level of detail stored in a fact table.
commonMistakes: Creating a single, large table mixing facts and dimensions (The One Big Table anti-pattern).
Designing a highly normalized schema for a data warehouse, which hurts performance.
Adding flags or indicators to a fact table instead of creating a separate dimension table.
